scenario: Multi-tenant cluster. Need per-tenant metrics -- how many instances, errors, throughput per tenant. Wonders setup.
question: Per-tenant metrics?

options.text: Per-tenant metrics are achievable through multiple mechanisms. increment()), enabling PromQL queries like sum by (tenant) (process_instances_total). Camunda's built-in Zeebe metrics may include tenant context in Self-Managed deployments with multi-tenancy enabled. The Operate REST API supports per-tenant filtering for process instance counts, incident counts, and other aggregates. Grafana dashboards with tenant variable selectors provide self-service per-tenant visibility.

Cluster-wide only -- this is only partially correct for default configurations. Without additional setup, Camunda's Prometheus metrics are cluster-wide aggregates. Custom workers and Connectors can emit tenant-labeled metrics using Micrometer or Prometheus client libraries. The Operate REST API also supports per-tenant queries that can be scraped by a metrics pipeline. Claiming metrics are cluster-wide only overlooks these tenant-aware patterns.

Per-tenant metrics via label dimensions: Prometheus-compatible metrics (emitted by custom workers or Connectors) can include a tenant label, enabling Grafana queries like sum by (tenant) (process_instances_total) to break down throughput, error rates, and active instances per tenant. Camunda's built-in Zeebe metrics may or may not include tenant labels by default (verify per version); custom worker code can emit supplemental metrics with tenant context using Micrometer tags. The Operate API supports per-tenant filters for programmatic aggregation.

Aggregate manually -- this is only partially workable as a one-off approach. Manually querying the Operate API for each tenant and summing counts is feasible for infrequent reporting but cannot provide real-time dashboards, does not integrate with alerting pipelines, and scales poorly with the number of tenants and metrics required. The recommended approach is systematic label-based metric emission (tenant as a Prometheus label) combined with Grafana dashboards, which enables continuous, automated per-tenant visibility without manual aggregation steps.

a.text: Incorrect. Per-tenant metrics: Prometheus labels: Standard label dimension: ` process_instances_total{tenant="A", process="order"} 1234 process_instances_total{tenant="B", process="order"} 567 Query: promql sum by (tenant) (process_instances_total) Aggregate per tenant. Camunda built-in metrics: Verify per version: Some metrics include tenant label: e.g., zeebe_process_instances_created_total{tenant=...}. Verify Camunda 8 emits: per metric type. Worker / Connector custom metrics: java // Pseudocode Counter ordersProcessed = Counter.builder("orders.processed") .tag("tenant", tenant) .register(registry); ordersProcessed.increment(); Workers emit with tenant tag. For dashboards: Grafana: Variable for tenant: dropdown selection. Filter queries: by selected tenant. Per-tenant view: same dashboard, different tenant. Multi-tenant SaaS dashboard: Tenant compares. Top tenants by usage. Anomaly detection. For Orchestration Cluster REST API (8.8 canonical): POST /process-instances/search Body: {"filter": {"tenantId": "A"}} Legacy GET /v1/process-instances/statistics` (Operate v1) is deprecated in 8.8. For business metrics: Worker emits: tenant-tagged counters. Per-tenant: order count, revenue, etc. Aggregate: per BI tool. For SLA per tenant: SLA breach rate: per tenant. Compliance per tenant. For billing: Per-tenant usage: metering. Cost allocation. For capacity planning: Per-tenant trends: growth. Resource consumption per tenant. For alerting: Per-tenant alerts: e.g., tenant A errors high. Don't alert globally when one tenant has issue. Implementation considerations: High cardinality: Many tenants = many label combinations. Prometheus performance: heavy. Cardinality bombs: avoid unbounded labels. For 100+ tenants: Aggregation strategies: top-N reporting. Sampling: not every tenant in every dashboard. For tenant-scoped queries: Tenant ID always in queries: enforce. Aggregations per tenant: standard. For audit + compliance: Per-tenant audit trail. Compliance reports per tenant. For chargeback: Tenant pays for usage. Metering: detailed. Invoicing: based on metrics. For Camunda 8 Self-Managed: Enable tenant labels if not default. Configure Prometheus scrape. Build Grafana dashboards. For SaaS: Per-cluster metrics: typically. Tenant within cluster: verify support. Limitations: Metric cardinality: limit tenants per label. Storage: more dimensions = more storage. Best practices: Label metrics with tenant: from start. Monitor cardinality: avoid bombs. Per-tenant alerts: don't drown signal. Aggregate views + drilldowns. Anti-patterns: No tenant label: can't filter. Tenant + many other labels: cardinality explosion. Cluster-only metrics: blind to per-tenant issues. For the scenario (per-tenant metrics): Enable tenant labels. Workers emit with tenant. Dashboards: filter by tenant. Alerts: per-tenant scoped. For multi-cluster multi-tenant: Combine cluster + tenant labels. Federated dashboards. For privacy: Tenant metrics don't leak data: just counts / aggregates. Compliance considerations: per regulation.

findings.message: option text contains its own verdict/rationale
